          V-223072 - Internet Explorer must be set to disallow users to add/delete sites.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223072r960963_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI318-IE11
          Identities:SV-59479
          V-46615
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: This setting prevents users from adding sites to various security zones. Users should not be able to add sites to different zones, as this could allow them to bypass security controls of the system. If you do not configure this policy setting, users will be able to add or remove sites from the Trusted Sites and Restricted Sites zones at will and change settings in the Local Intranet zone. This configuration could allow sites that host malicious mobile code to be added to these zones, and users could execute the code.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer 'Security Zones: Do not allow users to add/delete sites' must be 'Enabled'. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings Criteria: If the value "Security_zones_map_edit" is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer 'Security Zones: Do not allow users to add/delete sites' to 'Enabled'.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:419
          Definition Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Add/Delete Sites
          Description:The Security Zones: Do not allow users to add/delete sites setting prevents users from adding or removing sites from security zones. A security zone is a group of Web sites with the same security level.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Add/Delete Sites)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:41900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Add/Delete Sites
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:41900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Add/Delete Sites
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'Security_zones_map_edit'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:41900 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Add/Delete Sites
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Security_zones_map_edit'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223073 - Internet Explorer must be configured to disallow users to change policies.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223073r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI319-IE11
          Identities:SV-59481
          V-46617
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Users who change their Internet Explorer security settings could enable the execution of dangerous types of code from the Internet and websites listed in the Restricted Sites zone in the browser. This setting prevents users from changing the Internet Explorer policies on the machine. Policy changes should be made by administrators only, so this setting should be enabled. If you enable this policy setting, you disable the "Custom level" button and "Security" level for this zone slider on the Security tab in the Internet Options dialog box. If this policy setting is disabled or not configured, users will be able to change the settings for security zones. It prevents users from changing security zone policy settings that are established by the administrator.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer 'Security Zones: Do not allow users to change policies' must be 'Enabled'. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings Criteria: If the value "Security_options_edit" is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer 'Security Zones: Do not allow users to change policies' to 'Enabled'.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:418
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI319-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Change Policies
          Description:The Security Zones: Do not allow users to change policies setting prevents users from changing security zone settings. A security zone is a group of Web sites with the same security level.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Change Policies)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:41800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Change Policies
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:41800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Change Policies
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'Security_options_edit'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:41800 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Do Not Allow Users to Change Policies
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Security_options_edit'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223074 - Internet Explorer must be configured to use machine settings.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223074r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI320-IE11
          Identities:SV-59483
          V-46619
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Users who change their Internet Explorer security settings could enable the execution of dangerous types of code from the Internet and websites listed in the Restricted Sites zone in the browser. This setting enforces consistent security zone settings to all users of the computer. Security zones control browser behavior at various websites and it is desirable to maintain a consistent policy for all users of a machine. This policy setting affects how security zone changes apply to different users. If you enable this policy setting, changes that one user makes to a security zone will apply to all users of that computer. If this policy setting is disabled or not configured, users of the same computer are allowed to establish their own security zone settings.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer 'Security Zones: Use only machine settings' must be 'Enabled'. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings Criteria: If the value "Security_HKLM_only" is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer 'Security Zones: Use only machine settings' to 'Enabled'.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:417
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI320-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Use Only Machine Settings
          Description:Applies security zone information to all users of the same computer. A security zone is a group of Web sites with the same security level. If you enable this policy, changes that the user makes to a security zone will apply to all users of that computer.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Use Only Machine Settings)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:41700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Use Only Machine Settings
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:41700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Use Only Machine Settings
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'Security_HKLM_only'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:41700 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Security Zones: Use Only Machine Settings
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Security_HKLM_only'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223075 - Security checking features must be enforced.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223075r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI325-IE11
          Identities:SV-59485
          V-46621
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: This policy setting turns off the Security Settings Check feature, which checks Internet Explorer security settings to determine when the settings put Internet Explorer at risk. If you enable this policy setting, the security settings check will not be performed. If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the security settings check will be performed.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> 'Turn off the Security Settings Check feature' must be 'Disabled'. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Security Criteria: If the value "DisableSecuritySettingsCheck" is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> 'Turn off the Security Settings Check feature' to 'Disabled'.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:457
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI325-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Turn off the Security Settings Check feature
          Description:This policy setting turns off the Security Settings Check feature, which checks Internet Explorer security settings to determine when the settings put Internet Explorer at risk.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Turn off the Security Settings Check feature)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:45700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Turn off the Security Settings Check feature
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:45700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Turn off the Security Settings Check feature
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableSecuritySettingsCheck'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:45700 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Turn off the Security Settings Check feature
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Security\DisableSecuritySettingsCheck'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223076 - Software must be disallowed to run or install with invalid signatures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223076r961092_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI350-IE11
          Identities:SV-59489
          V-46625
          CCI-001170 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (4).1 (iii) (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (4))
          Description: Microsoft ActiveX controls and file downloads often have digital signatures attached that certify the file's integrity and the identity of the signer (creator) of the software. Such signatures help ensure unmodified software is downloaded and the user can positively identify the signer to determine whether you trust them enough to run their software.
          Check Text:Note: Some legitimate software and controls may have an invalid signature. You should carefully test such software in isolation before it is allowed to be used on an organization's network.

          The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Advanced Page -> 'Allow software to run or install even if the signature is invalid' must be 'Disabled'. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Download Criteria: If the value "RunInvalidSignatures" is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Advanced Page -> 'Allow software to run or install even if the signature is invalid' to 'Disabled'.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:459
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI350-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Allow Software to Run or Install Even if the Signature is Invalid
          Description:Microsoft ActiveX controls and file downloads often have digital signatures attached that vouch for both the file's integrity and the identity of the signer (creator) of the software. Such signatures help ensure that unmodified.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Allow Software to Run or Install Even if the Signature is Invalid)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:45900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Allow Software to Run or Install Even if the Signature is Invalid
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:45900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Allow Software to Run or Install Even if the Signature is Invalid
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Download'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'RunInvalidSignatures'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:45900 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Allow Software to Run or Install Even if the Signature is Invalid
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Download\RunInvalidSignatures'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223077 - The 64-bit tab processes, when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows, must be turned on.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223077r961131_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI356-IE11
          Identities:SV-59861
          V-46995
          CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
          Description: This policy setting determines whether Internet Explorer 11 uses 64-bit processes (for greater security) or 32-bit processes (for greater compatibility) when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows.Important: Some ActiveX controls and toolbars may not be available when 64-bit processes are used. If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer 11 will use 64-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows. If you disable this policy setting, Internet Explorer 11 will use 32-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows. If you don't configure this policy setting, users can turn this feature on or off using Internet Explorer settings. This feature is turned off by default.
          Check Text:Note: If McAfee ENS Web Control is being used, this is Not Applicable.

          The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer-> Internet Control Panel -> Advanced Page 'Turn on 64-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows' must be 'Enabled'. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main Criteria: If the value "Isolation64Bit" is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer-> Internet Control Panel -> Advanced Page 'Turn on 64-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows' to 'Enabled'.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:564
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI356-IE11 - The 64-bit tab processes, when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows, must be turned on
          Description:This policy setting determines whether Internet Explorer 11 uses 64-bit processes (for greater security) or 32-bit processes (for greater compatibility) when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows.Important: Some ActiveX controls and toolbars may not be available when 64-bit processes are used.If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer 11 will use 64-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows.If you disable this policy setting, Internet Explorer 11 will use 32-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows.If you don't configure this policy setting, users can turn this feature on or off using Internet Explorer settings. This feature is turned off by default.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Administrative Templates\Windows Components\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page Turn on 64-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:56400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Administrative Templates\Windows Components\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page Turn on 64-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:56400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Administrative Templates\Windows Components\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page Turn on 64-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'Isolation64Bit'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:56400 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Administrative Templates\Windows Components\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page Turn on 64-bit tab processes when running in Enhanced Protected Mode on 64-bit versions of Windows
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main\Isolation64Bit'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223079 - Checking for signatures on downloaded programs must be enforced.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223079r960954_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI370-IE11
          Identities:SV-59497
          V-46633
          CCI-001749 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-5 (3))
          Description: This policy setting allows you to manage whether Internet Explorer checks for digital signatures (which identifies the publisher of signed software and verifies it has not been modified or tampered with) on user computers before downloading executable programs. If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer will check the digital signatures of executable programs and display their identities before downloading them to the user computers. If you disable this policy setting, Internet Explorer will not check the digital signatures of executable programs or display their identities before downloading them to the user computers. If you do not configure this policy, Internet Explorer will not check the digital signatures of executable programs or display their identities before downloading them to the user computers.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Advanced Page -> 'Check for signatures on downloaded programs' must be 'Enabled'. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Download Criteria: If the value "CheckExeSignatures" is REG_SZ = yes, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Advanced Page -> 'Check for signatures on downloaded programs' to 'Enabled'.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:462
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI370-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Check for Signature on Downloaded Programs
          Description:This policy setting allows you to manage whether Internet Explorer checks for digital signatures (which identifies the publisher of signed software and verifies it hasn't been modified or tampered with) on user computers before downloading executable programs.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Check for Signature on Downloaded Programs)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:46200 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Check for Signature on Downloaded Programs
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:46200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Check for Signature on Downloaded Programs
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Download'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'CheckExeSignatures'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:46200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Advanced Page \Check for Signature on Downloaded Programs
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to 'yes'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Download\CheckExeSignatures'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223080 - All network paths (UNCs) for Intranet sites must be disallowed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223080r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI375-IE11
          Identities:SV-59499
          V-46635
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Some UNC paths could refer to servers not managed by the organization, which means they could host malicious content; and therefore, it is safest to not include all UNC paths in the Intranet Sites zone. This policy setting controls whether URLs representing UNCs are mapped into the local Intranet security zone. If you enable this policy setting, all network paths are mapped into the Intranet Zone. If you disable this policy setting, network paths are not necessarily mapped into the Intranet Zone (other rules might map one there). If you do not configure this policy setting, users choose whether network paths are mapped into the Intranet Zone.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> 'Intranet Sites: Include all network paths (UNCs)' must be 'Disabled'. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\ZoneMap Criteria: If the value "UNCAsIntranet" is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> 'Intranet Sites: Include all network paths (UNCs)' to 'Disabled'.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:463
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI375-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Intranet Sites: Include all network paths (UNCs)
          Description:This policy setting controls whether URLs representing UNCs are mapped into the local Intranet security zone. If you enable this policy setting, all network paths are mapped into the Intranet Zone.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Intranet Sites: Include all network paths (UNCs))
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:46300 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Intranet Sites: Include all network paths (UNCs)
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:46300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Intranet Sites: Include all network paths (UNCs)
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\ZoneMap'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'UNCAsIntranet'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:46300 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Intranet Sites: Include all network paths (UNCs)
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\ZoneMap\UNCAsIntranet'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223081 - Script-initiated windows without size or position constraints must be disallowed (Internet zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223081r960963_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI385-IE11
          Identities:SV-59501
          V-46637
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: This policy setting allows you to manage restrictions on script-initiated pop-up windows and windows including the title and status bars. If you enable this policy setting, Windows Restrictions security will not apply in this zone. The security zone runs without the added layer of security provided by this feature. If you disable this policy setting, the possible harmful actions contained in script-initiated pop-up windows and windows including the title and status bars cannot be run. This Internet Explorer security feature will be on in this zone as dictated by the Scripted Windows Security Restrictions feature control setting for the process. If you do not configure this policy setting, the possible harmful actions contained in script-initiated pop-up windows and windows including the title and status bars cannot be run. This Internet Explorer security feature will be on in this zone as dictated by the Scripted Windows Security Restrictions feature control setting for the process.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Allow script-initiated windows without size or position constraints' must be 'Enabled', and 'Disable' selected from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3 Criteria: If the value "2102" is REG_DWORD = 3, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Allow script-initiated windows without size or position constraints' to 'Enabled', and select 'Disable' from the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:464
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI385-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Allow script-initiated windows without size or position constraints
          Description:This policy setting allows you to manage restrictions on script-initiated pop-up windows and windows that include the title and status bars. If you enable this policy setting, Windows Restrictions security will not apply in this zone.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Allow script-initiated windows without size or position constraints)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:46400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Allow script-initiated windows without size or position constraints
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:46400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Allow script-initiated windows without size or position constraints
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '2102'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:46400 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Allow script-initiated windows without size or position constraints
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '3'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3\2102'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223082 - Script-initiated windows without size or position constraints must be disallowed (Restricted Sites zone).

If you enable this policy setting, Windows Restrictions security will not apply in this zone. The security zone runs without the added layer of security provided by this feature. If you disable this policy setting, the possible harmful actions contained in script-initiated pop-up windows and windows including the title and status bars cannot be run. This Internet Explorer security feature will be on in this zone as dictated by the Scripted Windows Security Restrictions feature control setting for the process.           V-223083 - Scriptlets must be disallowed (Internet zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223083r960963_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI395-IE11
          Identities:SV-59505
          V-46641
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: This policy setting allows you to manage whether scriptlets can be allowed. Scriptlets hosted on sites located in this zone are more likely to contain malicious code. If you enable this policy setting, users will be able to run scriptlets. If you disable this policy setting, users will not be able to run scriptlets. If you do not configure this policy setting, a scriptlet can be enabled or disabled by the user.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Allow Scriptlets' must be 'Enabled', and 'Disable' from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3 Criteria: If the value "1209" is REG_DWORD = 3, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Allow Scriptlets' to 'Enabled', and select 'Disable' from the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:466
          Definition Result:false

          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Allow Scriptlets)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:46600 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Allow Scriptlets
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:46600 (registry_object)

          V-223084 - Automatic prompting for file downloads must be disallowed (Internet zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223084r960963_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI415-IE11
          Identities:SV-59507
          V-46643
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: This policy setting determines whether users will be prompted for non user-initiated file downloads. Regardless of this setting, users will receive file download dialogs for user-initiated downloads. Users may accept downloads that they did not request, and those downloaded files may include malicious code. If you enable this setting, users will receive a file download dialog for automatic download attempts. If you disable or do not configure this setting, file downloads that are not user-initiated will be blocked, and users will see the information bar instead of the file download dialog. Users can then click the information bar to allow the file download prompt.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Automatic prompting for file downloads' must be 'Enabled', and 'Disable' selected from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3 Criteria: If the value "2200" is REG_DWORD = 3, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Automatic prompting for file downloads' to 'Enabled', and select 'Disable' from the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:467
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI415-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Automatic prompting for file downloads
          Description:This policy setting determines whether users will be prompted for non user-initiated file downloads. Regardless of this setting, users will receive file download dialogs for user-initiated downloads.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Automatic prompting for file downloads)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:46700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Automatic prompting for file downloads
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:46700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Automatic prompting for file downloads
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '2200'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:46700 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Automatic prompting for file downloads
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '3'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3\2200'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223085 - Java permissions must be disallowed (Local Machine zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223085r960963_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI425-IE11
          Identities:SV-59509
          V-46645
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Java applications could contain malicious code. This policy setting allows you to manage permissions for Java applets. If you enable this policy setting, options can be chosen from the drop-down box. Use of the Custom permission will control permissions settings individually. Use of the Low Safety permission enables applets to perform all operations. Use of the Medium Safety permission enables applets to run in their sandbox (an area in memory outside of which the program cannot make calls), plus adds capabilities like scratch space (a safe and secure storage area on the client computer) and a user-controlled file I/O. Use of the High Safety permission enables applets to run in their sandbox. If you disable this policy setting, Java applets cannot run. If you do not configure this policy setting, the permission is set to High Safety.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Local Machine Zone -> 'Java permissions' must be 'Enabled', and 'Disable Java' selected from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following keys: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\0 Criteria: If the value "1C00" is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Local Machine Zone -> 'Java permissions' to 'Enabled', and select 'Disable Java' from the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:468
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI425-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Local Machine Zone\Java permissions
          Description:This policy setting allows you to manage permissions for Java applets. If you enable this policy setting, you can choose options from the drop-down box. Custom, to control permissions settings individually.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Local Machine Zone\Java permissions)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:46800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Local Machine Zone\Java permissions
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:46800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Local Machine Zone\Java permissions
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\0'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '1C00'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:46800 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Local Machine Zone\Java permissions
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\0\1C00'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223086 - Anti-Malware programs against ActiveX controls must be run for the Local Machine zone.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223086r961086_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI426-IE11
          Identities:SV-59869
          V-47003
          CCI-001662 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: SC-18 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-18 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-18 (1))
          Description: This policy setting determines whether Internet Explorer runs Anti-Malware programs against ActiveX controls, to check if they're safe to load on pages. If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer won't check with your Anti-Malware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control. If you disable this policy setting, Internet Explorer always checks with your Anti-Malware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control. If you don't configure this policy setting, Internet Explorer won't check with your Anti-Malware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control. Users can turn this behavior on or off, using Internet Explorer Security settings.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer-> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page ->Local Machine Zone 'Don't run antimalware programs against ActiveX controls' must be 'Enabled' and 'Disable' selected in the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\0 Criteria: If the value "270C" is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer-> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Local Machine Zone 'Don't run antimalware programs against ActiveX controls' to 'Enabled' and select 'Disable' in the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:567
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI426-IE11 - Antimalware programs against ActiveX controls must be run for the Local Machine zone
          Description:This policy setting determines whether Internet Explorer runs antimalware programs against ActiveX controls, to check if they're safe to load on pages. If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer won't check with your antimalware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control.If you disable this policy setting, Internet Explorer always checks with your antimalware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control.If you don't configure this policy setting, Internet Explorer won't check with your antimalware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control. Users can turn this behavior on or off, using Internet Explorer Security settings.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Administrative Templates\Windows Components\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Local Machine Zone Don't run antimalware programs against ActiveX controls)

          Result:false

          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:56700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Administrative Templates\Windows Components\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Local Machine Zone Don't run antimalware programs against ActiveX controls
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\0'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '270C'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:56700 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Administrative Templates\Windows Components\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Local Machine Zone Don't run antimalware programs against ActiveX controls

Use of the Low Safety permission enables applets to perform all operations. Use of the Medium Safety permission enables applets to run in their sandbox (an area in memory outside of which the program cannot make calls), plus adds capabilities like scratch space (a safe and secure storage area on the client computer) and a user-controlled file I/O. If you do not configure this policy setting, the permission is set to High Safety.

          V-223089 - Java permissions must be disallowed (Locked Down Trusted Sites zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223089r960963_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI440-IE11
          Identities:SV-59517
          V-46653
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Java applications could contain malicious code; sites located in this security zone are more likely to be hosted by malicious individuals. This policy setting allows you to manage permissions for Java applets. If you enable this policy setting, options can be chosen from the drop-down box. Use of the Custom permission will control permissions settings individually. Use of the Low Safety permission enables applets to perform all operations. Use of the Medium Safety permission enables applets to run in their sandbox (an area in memory outside of which the program cannot make calls), plus adds capabilities like scratch space (a safe and secure storage area on the client computer) and a user-controlled file I/O. Use of the High Safety permission enables applets to run in their sandbox. If you disable this policy setting, Java applets cannot run. If you do not configure this policy setting, the permission is set to High Safety.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Locked-Down Trusted Sites Zone -> 'Java permissions' must be 'Enabled', and 'Disable Java' selected from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following keys: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Lockdown_Zones\2 Criteria: If the value "1C00" is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Locked-Down Trusted Sites Zone -> 'Java permissions' to 'Enabled', and select 'Disable Java' from the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0

          V-223091 - XAML files must be disallowed (Internet zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223091r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI455-IE11
          Identities:SV-59529
          V-46665
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: These are eXtensible Application Markup Language (XAML) files. XAML is an XML-based declarative markup language commonly used for creating rich user interfaces and graphics that leverage the Windows Presentation Foundation. If you enable this policy setting and the drop-down box is set to Enable, XAML files will be automatically loaded inside Internet Explorer. Users will not be able to change this behavior. If the drop-down box is set to Prompt, users will receive a prompt for loading XAML files. If you disable this policy setting, XAML files will not be loaded inside Internet Explorer. Users will not be able to change this behavior. If you do not configure this policy setting, users will have the freedom to decide whether to load XAML files inside Internet Explorer.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Allow loading of XAML files' must be 'Enabled', and 'Disable' selected from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3 Criteria: If the value "2402" is REG_DWORD = 3, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Allow loading of XAML files' to 'Enabled', and select 'Disable' from the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:474
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI455-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Loose or un-compiled XAML files
          Description:These are eXtensible Application Markup Language (XAML) files. XAML is an XML-based declarative markup language commonly used for creating rich user interfaces and graphics that leverage the Windows Presentation Foundation.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Loose or un-compiled XAML files)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:47400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Loose or un-compiled XAML files
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:47400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Loose or un-compiled XAML files
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '2402'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:47400 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Loose or un-compiled XAML files
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '3'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3\2402'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223092 - XAML files must be disallowed (Restricted Sites zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223092r961863_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI460-IE11
          Identities:SV-59533
          V-46669
          CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: These are eXtensible Application Markup Language (XAML) files. XAML is an XML-based declarative markup language commonly used for creating rich user interfaces and graphics that leverage the Windows Presentation Foundation. If you enable this policy setting and the drop-down box is set to Enable, XAML files will be automatically loaded inside Internet Explorer. Users will not be able to change this behavior. If the drop-down box is set to Prompt, users will receive a prompt for loading XAML files. If you disable this policy setting, XAML files will not be loaded inside Internet Explorer. Users will not be able to change this behavior. If you do not configure this policy setting, users will have the freedom to decide whether to load XAML files inside Internet Explorer.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Restricted Sites Zone -> 'Allow loading of XAML files' must be 'Enabled', and 'Disable' selected from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\4 Criteria: If the value "2402" is REG_DWORD = 3, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Restricted Sites Zone -> 'Allow loading of XAML files' to 'Enabled', and select 'Disable' from the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:475
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI460-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Restricted Sites Zone\Loose XAML files

          Tests:

            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Restricted Sites Zone\Loose XAML files)

          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:47500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Restricted Sites Zone\Loose XAML files
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\4'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '2402'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:47500 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Restricted Sites Zone\Loose or XAML files
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '3'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\4\2402'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223093 - Protected Mode must be enforced (Internet zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223093r961131_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI485-IE11
          Identities:SV-59545
          V-46681
          CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
          Description: Protected Mode protects Internet Explorer from exploited vulnerabilities by reducing the locations Internet Explorer can write to in the registry and the file system. If you enable this policy setting, Protected Mode will be turned on. Users will not be able to turn off Protected Mode. If you disable this policy setting, Protected Mode will be turned off. It will revert to Internet Explorer 6 behavior that allows for Internet Explorer to write to the registry and the file system. Users will not be able to turn on Protected Mode. If you do not configure this policy, users will be able to turn on or off Protected Mode.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Turn on Protected Mode' must be 'Enabled', and 'Enable' selected from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3 Criteria: If the value "2500" is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Turn on Protected Mode' to 'Enabled', and select 'Enable' from the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:480
          Definition Result:false
          Title:DTBI485-IE11 - Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Turn On Protected Mode
          Description:The 'Turn on Protected Mode' setting should be configured correctly for the Internet Zone.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Turn On Protected Mode)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:tst:48000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Turn On Protected Mode
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:obj:48000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Turn On Protected Mode
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '2500'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:ste:48000 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Turn On Protected Mode
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • for allvalue, the following must be true:
            • value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3\2500'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-223094 - Protected Mode must be enforced (Restricted Sites zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223094r961131_rule

          Description: Protected Mode protects Internet Explorer from exploited vulnerabilities by reducing the locations Internet Explorer can write to in the registry and the file system. If you enable this policy setting, Protected Mode will be turned on. Users will not be able to turn off Protected Mode. If you disable this policy setting, Protected Mode will be turned off. It will revert to Internet Explorer 6 behavior that allows for Internet Explorer to write to the registry and the file system. Users will not be able to turn on Protected Mode. If you do not configure this policy, users will be able to turn on or off Protected Mode.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Restricted Sites Zone -> 'Turn on Protected Mode' must be 'Enabled', and 'Enable' selected from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\4 Criteria: If the value "2500" is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Restricted Sites Zone -> 'Turn on Protected Mode' to 'Enabled' and select 'Enable', from the drop-down box.

          V-223095 - Pop-up Blocker must be enforced (Internet zone).

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.fso_rule_SV-223095r960963_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:DTBI495-IE11
          Identities:SV-59553
          V-46689
          CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: This policy setting allows you to manage whether unwanted pop-up windows appear. Pop-up windows that are opened when the end user clicks a link are not blocked. If you enable this policy setting, most unwanted pop-up windows are prevented from appearing. If you disable this policy setting, pop-up windows are not prevented from appearing. If you do not configure this policy setting, most unwanted pop-up windows are prevented from appearing.
          Check Text:The policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Use Pop-up Blocker' must be 'Enabled', and 'Enable' selected from the drop-down box. Procedure: Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Zones\3 Criteria: If the value "1809" is REG_DWORD = 0, this is not a finding.
          Fix Text:Set the policy value for Computer Configuration -> Administrative Templates -> Windows Components -> Internet Explorer -> Internet Control Panel -> Security Page -> Internet Zone -> 'Use Pop-up Blocker' to 'Enabled', and select 'Enable' from the drop-down box.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Internet Explorer 11
          Identifier:4095
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.fso.ie:def:482
          Definition Result:false

            • false (Computer Configuration\Network\Internet Explorer\Internet Control Panel\Security Page\Internet Zone\Use Pop-up Blocker)
